title: Status Column Summary Footer - Colour order

posts.author: FredBrosseau (Frederic Brosseau)
posts.cookedHtml:
I was wondering what determines the order in which the colours appear, from left to right, in the status column summary footer ?

Based on the screenshot below, I can’t figure out how the order is determined.

It’s not based on the number of pulses per status, neither is it based on the order of appearence in the dropdown menu of the status column (which would be my preference) nor is it based on the default order configured in the admin section under Customization/Boards.

image.png868×558 31.2 KB

Thanks for your help !

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml: Good question. I was wondering the same thing.

posts.author: Krishele (Krishele Guzman)
posts.cookedHtml: woah! thought it was based on order of status labels, but never looked into it I guess. Would love to see an answer on the current rational. Also, as a future update would be nice to have the option to choose based on # of pulses per status or order of the status dropdown.

posts.author: Thean (Thean Oberhollenzer)
posts.cookedHtml: If you click on “Add / Edit Labels” you can change the order however you want.

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
If you click on “Add / Edit Labels” you can change the order however you want.

Understood but the question refers to the column summary at the bottom of the status column - not the order of the Status label definitions.

See “Add a footer to see progress”

Support

The Status Column

3 min read Feature   Here at monday.com, we strive to give you an intuitive, visual platform that manages everything in one place. Our Status Column is a key factor in helping you plan, organ...

The order of the colors in the column summary (previously known as the footer)

bears no apparent relationship to the order of the colors in the Status column above

posts.author: FredBrosseau (Frederic Brosseau)
posts.cookedHtml:
And again, the question is really what determines the orde in which the colours are displayed in the column summary.

In the screenshot, in the column Priority A, you can see that the Red (Priority 1) is not the first colour in the summary but it is the category with the most pulses (3) and it is the first choice in the dropdown.

In the screenshot, in the column Priority B, you can see that the order in the dropdown is different but it has no effect on the order in which the colours are displayed in the column summary.

My expectation was that colours would be displayed in order of number of pulses or in the order determined in the dropdown list, so that the user has a frame of reference when looking at the summary line.

My preference would be the order determined in the dropdown list.

posts.author: Lea-monday.com (Léa )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey guys!

The order of the colors in the status footer is determined by the order of the status in the admin section.
If you are an admin of the account, click on your admin section > customization > boards.
You will be able to determine the status labels.
The order you set up will be the same order as in your footer.

@FredBrosseau beautiful board btw!

Hope this helps
Lea

posts.author: Krishele (Krishele Guzman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is there any thoughts of moving the column summary order management to weighted by usage or the order on the specific board. It seems TOO big brother to maintain this in the admin.

We have many different teams and individuals with boards and I imagine everyone would want it a specific way to the board’s use case.

Seems almost random.
